title: nie-bot: BotConfig struct + TOML/env loading
description:
What: Define BotConfig struct with TOML deserialization and env-var override.

Algorithm:
1. Create bot/src/config.rs with:
   - #[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Default)] pub struct BotConfig { relay: Option<String>, keyfile: Option<PathBuf>, log_level: Option<String>, proxy: Option<String>, on_message_hook: Option<String>, auto_payment_address: bool }
   - pub fn load(config_dir: &Path) -> Result<BotConfig> — reads bot.toml via toml::from_str; returns default if file absent
   - pub fn resolve(cli_flags: &CliBotArgs, file: BotConfig) -> ResolvedBotConfig — merges: CLI > env > file > compiled default
   - ResolvedBotConfig with all required fields (relay: String, keyfile: PathBuf, etc.)
2. RELAY env var overrides relay field; KEYFILE env var overrides keyfile; LOG_LEVEL env var overrides log_level
3. relay default: ws://127.0.0.1:3210/ws; keyfile default: data_dir/identity.key; log_level default: warn

Defensive:
- validate relay URL scheme is ws:// or wss://; reject other schemes with BotError::Config
- validate keyfile path is not empty
- validate log_level is a valid tracing filter string

Agent team: I (implementer). V (validator) runs cargo build + tests.
Tests: parse TOML with all fields; parse empty TOML returns defaults; env var overrides file value; invalid relay scheme returns Err
acceptance_criteria: BotConfig parses valid TOML; invalid relay scheme returns BotError::Config; unit tests pass; clippy clean
